title: Fast Cash Auto / Raifinance
tagline: From generic WordPress loan page to brand-honest green-ground editorial. Three conversion hypotheses: 2-field micro-form, photo hero trust-first, and loan calculator entry point.

source.category: financial services · car title loans · direct lender

direction.phrase: Brand-honest green — the actual Fast Cash Auto brand colour (#539744) as the hero ground, not an invented amber. The brand is green: the hero is green. 'Green light — you're approved.' White body sections, warm readable Nunito Sans. Audience is financially stressed people who've been rejected by banks: they need warmth and trust, not dark editorial authority. Conversion goals baked in: 2-field micro-form, testimonials above fold, FAQ schema, LocalBusiness schema, sticky mobile CTA.
movements: colour correction: invented amber #D4820A → actual brand green #539744
ground correction: dark asphalt editorial → hero green (#3D6E33) + white body — 'green light, you're approved'
typography correction: Barlow Condensed ALL-CAPS (gym/energy-drink register) → Nunito Sans (warm, approachable, financial-service register)
register correction: dark editorial authority → warm local business credibility (15 years, real customers, Alberta)
conversion: generic hero → 2-field micro-form (phone + city) in the hero (A), photo hero with approval framing (B), interactive loan calculator (C)
trust: testimonials moved above the fold, immediately after hero — not buried below scroll
SEO: FAQ JSON-LD schema, LocalBusiness schema, local title tags (Edmonton Car Title Loans, Auto Equity Loans Calgary)
mobile: sticky bottom CTA bar on all three variants

seed.register: Warm authority — credit union, not fintech startup
seed.pickedBy: brand colour extraction — #539744 confirmed from live CSS

fontDeck.name: warm-approachable-financial
fontDeck.value: Nunito Sans 400/600/700/800/900 — humanist, readable, not cold or trendy
fontDeck.pickedBy: audience fit — stressed borrowers need warmth, not editorial weight

palette.name: Fast Cash Auto actual brand
palette.source: Extracted from live site CSS — theme accent #539744 confirmed as primary brand colour
brandFaithfulInversions: Green #539744 — the actual brand accent, confirmed from live CSS (not invented)
Dark green #3D6E33 — hero ground, deepened for contrast with white text
Forest #2A5120 — footer ground, darkest expression of brand green
Sage #F0F5EE — section alt ground, barely-green white that echoes the brand
Ink #1B2118 — near-black with green undertone for body text

originalCritique: Raifinance.com is a WordPress loan site using the Responsive Brix theme with its default coral/salmon accent overridden to forest green #539744. The site undersells the vehicle-equity mechanism — the actual differentiator that lets Fast Cash Auto approve every applicant a bank has rejected. The hero uses generic slider copy. No credit-check positioning is present above the fold. The design is cream-on-white with a WordPress default layout; it reads like a brand that doesn't know what it owns. A first redesign attempt (this session) introduced an invented amber colour and dark editorial aesthetics — both wrong for the brand and the audience. The brand is green. The hero should be green. The audience needs warmth, not authority.

variants.name: Green light · 2-field form
variants.thesis: The hero IS green — the brand colour as the first impression, reading as 'approved.' 2-field micro-form (phone + city) is the only ask in the hero. Testimonials land immediately below, above the first scroll. Tests whether lowest-friction entry (just phone number) maximises lead volume.
variants.fix: Green #3D6E33 hero ground — the brand colour is the first thing you see. Nunito Sans throughout. 2-field form card in the hero: phone + city → call back in minutes. Testimonials appear before the first scroll. FAQ JSON-LD + LocalBusiness schema. Sticky mobile CTA bar. Every headline passes specificity test.

variants.name: Photo hero · trust-forward
variants.thesis: 'When the bank said no, we said yes.' — leads with the applicant's emotional situation before asking for anything. Brand slider image as full-bleed photo hero. Human image in 'who we help' section. Tests whether emotional framing of the real situation converts better than A's product-first approach.
variants.fix: Brand slider_01.jpg as full-bleed hero with dark gradient overlay. Same green palette, same Nunito Sans. 'When the bank said no, we said yes.' as hero headline — names the actual situation the visitor is in. Who-we-help section uses the brand friends image. Full loan parameters card on hero panel. Tests warmth-first vs. form-first.

variants.name: Loan calculator · estimate first
variants.thesis: 'Find out what your car is worth in 60 seconds.' — the interactive estimator answers the visitor's first question (how much can I get?) before asking for contact info. Reduces anxiety. Tests whether giving the number first increases conversion rate vs. asking first.
variants.fix: Interactive loan estimator as the hero interaction. Year + make + km slider → estimated range ($X,XXX – $XX,XXX). After estimate shows, 'Get my confirmed quote' CTA appears. Same green palette, same Nunito Sans. Tests the hypothesis: does answering 'how much?' before asking for the phone number convert better?
